variableDefinitionForm.labelHint: Human-readable name shown in the UI
variableDefinitionForm.descriptionPlaceholder: The customer's email address for follow-up communication
variableDefinitionForm.descriptionHint: Instructions for the AI on how to extract this value from the conversation
variableDefinitionForm.requiredField: Required variable (must be extracted if mentioned)
variableDefinitionForm.mandatoryCollectionHint: Agent must actively ask for this field during the call
variableDefinitionForm.confirmWithCallerHint: Agent reads back the value and asks the caller to confirm (recommended for names, companies, and other values prone to STT errors)
variableDefinitionForm.validationRegexHint: Pattern to validate the value immediately
variableDefinitionForm.validationErrorHintHint: Shown to agent when validation fails
variableDefinitionForm.nameFormatError: Name must start with a letter and contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores

webhookConfig.title: Post-Call Webhook
webhookConfig.description: Send call data to an external URL after each call
webhookConfig.descriptionAfterCall: Send call data, variables and optionally transcript to an external URL after each call
webhookConfig.urlHint: POST endpoint called after every completed call
webhookConfig.noHeaders: Add custom headers like Authorization tokens
webhookConfig.includeTranscriptHint: Sends the full call transcript along in the payload

contextWebhook.title: Context Webhook
contextWebhook.description: Fetch external data when a call starts. The returned data can be used in your prompts using
contextWebhook.urlHint: POST request with caller info. Returns JSON with context data.
contextWebhook.timeoutHint: Max wait time before the call proceeds without context (1-30 seconds)
contextWebhook.expectedResponseHint: Return any JSON object. Fields become available as variables.

webhookTools.title: HTTP Webhook Tools
webhookTools.description: Define HTTP endpoints the assistant can call as tools during a conversation.
webhookTools.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: What this tool does and when to call it
webhookTools.noParameters: No parameters – the assistant calls the tool without arguments
webhookTools.deleteDescription: This tool will be removed from all assistants. This action cannot be undone.

endCall.title: End Call
endCall.description: Allow the assistant to hang up when the conversation is complete
endCall.instructionsPlaceholder: e.g., When the caller says goodbye, when all questions have been answered, or when you cannot help further

transferCall.title: Transfer Call
transferCall.description: Allow the assistant to transfer calls to a human agent
transferCall.instructionsPlaceholder: e.g., When the caller explicitly requests a human agent, or when the issue is too complex

takeNotes.title: Take Notes
takeNotes.description: Allow the assistant to save important notes during calls
takeNotes.instructionsPlaceholder: e.g., When the caller mentions action items, complaints, or important information that needs follow-up

bargeIn.title: Caller Interruptions (Barge-In)
bargeIn.description: Control if and how callers can interrupt the assistant while it is speaking
bargeIn.immediateDescription: Interrupts immediately when the caller starts speaking — fastest response using voice activity detection
bargeIn.minimumCharactersDescription: Automatically detects when the caller speaks and interrupts the assistant
bargeIn.manualDescription: Interruptions are only triggered via the API, not by caller speech
bargeIn.noneDescription: The assistant cannot be interrupted — audio plays fully without interruption
bargeIn.minCharsHint: How many characters the caller must speak before the interruption triggers
bargeIn.protectionPeriodHint: Delay before barge-in is allowed after the assistant starts speaking

landing.heroDescription: Build prompt-based voice AI agents on top of sipgate Flow. Define agent behavior with a system prompt, route calls visually with scenarios, go live in minutes. Self-hostable.
landing.pillar1Desc: MIT licensed. Full source code, host it yourself. No black box, no vendor lock-in, no opaque pricing.
landing.pillar2Desc: Agent behavior is a system prompt — free-form, not a rigid decision tree. Optionally route between multiple agents with a visual scenario builder.
landing.pillar3Desc: Built on sipgate Flow for European telephony. GDPR-compliant, German data centers, crystal-clear audio.
landing.step1Desc: Link your sipgate account and route a phone number to your agent. Existing numbers work too.
landing.step2Desc: Define your agent's persona, knowledge, and behavior in plain text. Optionally attach a knowledge base for RAG-powered answers.
landing.step2Detail: → OpenAI · Gemini · bring your own model
landing.step3Desc: Go live instantly. Monitor real-time transcripts, review call recordings, and tune your prompt from the dashboard.
landing.capabilitiesDesc: Flow-IO ships with the features voice agent builders actually need — without forcing you into a specific platform or workflow.
landing.cap1Desc: Switch between OpenAI GPT-4, Google Gemini, or plug in your own provider. Never locked into a single model or pricing tier.
landing.cap2Desc: Upload documents and let your agent answer from them. Chunking, embeddings, and pgvector similarity search built in.
landing.cap3Desc: Extend your agent with MCP servers. CRM lookups, calendar bookings, custom API calls — your agent handles it all mid-call.
landing.cap4Desc: Full call transcripts, session recordings, and analytics. Understand exactly what your agent says and why.
landing.whyDescription: sipgate Agents are polished and curated — great for standard use cases. Flow-IO is for builders who need full control: custom prompts, any LLM, any integration.
landing.vs1Them: sipgate Agents: curated steps, constrained behavior
landing.vs1Us: Flow-IO: free-form system prompts — your agent does exactly what you tell it
landing.vs2Them: US-only carriers, data leaves Europe, opaque pricing
landing.vs2Us: sipgate backbone, GDPR-compliant, German data centers
landing.vs3Us: MIT licensed — clone, fork, deploy on your own infra
landing.ctaDescription: Clone the repo, deploy to your own infrastructure, and own your voice AI stack completely. Or use the hosted version to get started in seconds.
landing.selfHostNote: git clone · docker compose up · done
landing.builtWith: Built with Next.js, Supabase & sipgate Flow
